| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260527-122633-0e69 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T12:27:10+02:00 |

## Description

Add native actions for creating goals and tickets, claiming tickets, commenting, completing with evidence, and syncing, while routing every state mutation through the maat CLI.

## Acceptance

- Goal creation uses maat goal create with an outcome and shows the created ID.
- Ticket creation enforces description and acceptance criteria before invoking maat ticket create.
- Claim, comment, and complete actions use the existing ticket CLI commands and refresh visible state after success.

documents.title: Ticket: Harden signing notarization and permissions
documents.content:
# Ticket: Harden signing notarization and permissions

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260527-122710-9e6b |
| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260527-122633-0e69 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T12:27:10+02:00 |

## Description

Prepare the macOS app for trusted distribution with signed app and CLI binaries, notarization, limited filesystem access, and no credential storage.

## Acceptance

- The Tauri app and bundled maat binary are signed for macOS distribution.
- The app is notarized or has a documented notarization path.
- The app only requests storage repo access when the user selects a repo and relies on existing Git credential helpers instead of storing credentials.

documents.title: Ticket: Resolve first-release desktop product decisions
documents.content:
# Ticket: Resolve first-release desktop product decisions

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260527-122710-a980 |
| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260527-122633-0e69 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T12:27:10+02:00 |

## Description

Close the open product decisions from docs/macos-app-architecture.md before implementation begins in earnest.

## Acceptance

- The team decides whether first release installs only the app-private CLI or also prompts for terminal CLI installation.
- The team decides whether clone-storage support ships in the first release or starts with selecting/creating local storage.
- The team decides whether auto-push is exposed in first-run setup or only advanced settings.
- The team decides whether to embed a read-only terminal/TUI view or focus only on native CLI-backed views.

documents.title: Ticket: Define app and CLI update behavior
documents.content:
# Ticket: Define app and CLI update behavior

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260527-122710-bc86 |
| Project | maat |
| Goal | G-20260527-122633-0e69 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T12:27:10+02:00 |

## Description

Implement the update model for a Tauri app that ships with a bundled CLI while still allowing the app to know and verify the CLI it is using.

## Acceptance

- App updates can replace the bundled CLI and refresh the app-private installed CLI.
- The app records and displays the active CLI path and version.
- Advanced CLI self-update behavior through maat update --json is either implemented or explicitly deferred.

documents.title: Ticket: Add optional terminal CLI installation

## Description

Offer a setup or settings action that installs the same maat CLI for terminal use without making shell profile changes mandatory for the desktop app.

## Acceptance

- The desktop app remains usable with only the app-private CLI installed.
- A user can explicitly install or link maat into a PATH directory for terminal use.
- The UI explains the app-private CLI path and terminal CLI path as separate install targets.

# Ticket: Add SMTP IMAP and POP monitors

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260527-112346-811e |
| Project | orion |
| Goal | G-20260527-111626-e3b0 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T11:23:46+02:00 |

## Description

Implement Core worker mail protocol availability checks for SMTP, IMAP, and POP with TLS mode, banner or capability checks, and optional credential support later.

## Acceptance

- Mail monitors can check configured protocol endpoints, report banner/capability and TLS context, handle auth-disabled mode safely, and pass protocol runner tests.

documents.title: Ticket: Add Playwright transaction monitor
documents.content:
# Ticket: Add Playwright transaction monitor

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260527-112401-3ba3 |
| Project | orion |
| Goal | G-20260527-111626-e3b0 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T11:24:01+02:00 |

## Description

Implement Core worker browser transaction checks using Playwright with sandboxing, timeout budgets, secret handling, screenshots on failure, and artifact retention limits.

## Acceptance

- Playwright monitors can run bounded transactions, capture failure artifacts, redact secrets, avoid overloading the worker, and pass tests or smoke checks for success and failure flows.

documents.title: Ticket: Add covered incident suppression semantics
documents.content:
# Ticket: Add covered incident suppression semantics

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260527-112402-35db |
| Project | orion |
| Goal | G-20260527-112352-1456 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T11:24:02+02:00 |

## Description

Teach incident reconciliation to honor active coverage after a user marks an incident covered, suppress duplicate incident creation until recovery or expiry, and record coverage-related audit events.

## Acceptance

- A covered still-failing monitor does not immediately open a duplicate incident; coverage ends on recovery or expiry; incident events record suppressed-by-coverage behavior; Console shows coverage state and offers an end coverage or reopen path.

documents.title: Ticket: Add synthetic multi-step monitor
documents.content:
# Ticket: Add synthetic multi-step monitor

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260527-112412-1f05 |
| Project | orion |
| Goal | G-20260527-111626-e3b0 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T11:24:12+02:00 |

## Description

Implement Core worker synthetic multi-step checks for ordered API or browser steps with shared variables, assertions, stop-on-failure behavior, and bounded artifacts.

## Acceptance

- Synthetic monitors run ordered steps, preserve step-level results, fail with actionable context, respect timeout and artifact limits, and pass tests for success and step failure.

documents.title: Ticket: Expose incident evidence and raw report inspection
documents.content:
# Ticket: Expose incident evidence and raw report inspection

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260527-112420-91df |
| Project | orion |
| Goal | G-20260527-112410-6db8 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T11:24:20+02:00 |

## Description

Extend incident detail responses and Console views with normalized triggering report, latest report, related incidents, safe raw payload inspection, and timeline links back to reports or deliveries.

## Acceptance

- Incident detail shows trigger versus latest evidence; raw monitor report payloads are inspectable from incident and monitor history; timeline rows link to the relevant report or alert delivery; tests cover response shape and safe payload exposure.

documents.title: Ticket: Add incident analytics and lifecycle filters
documents.content:
# Ticket: Add incident analytics and lifecycle filters

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260527-112437-37f1 |
| Project | orion |
| Goal | G-20260527-112426-5108 |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T11:24:37+02:00 |

## Description

Add Core filters and aggregate data for incident resolution kind, actor, covered state, recurring monitor failures, notification reliability, and mean time to acknowledge or resolve; surface the most useful insights in Console.

## Acceptance

- Incidents can be filtered by resolution kind, actor, and covered state; Core exposes aggregate metrics for recurring failures and lifecycle timing; Console summary or detail views show actionable incident insights without replacing the operational tables.

documents.title: Ticket: Spawn Core monitor milestone agents
documents.content:
# Ticket: Spawn Core monitor milestone agents

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260527-122246-1e31 |
| Project | orion |
| Goal | G-20260526-215257-e70b |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T12:22:46+02:00 |

## Description

Spawn one supervising agent per Core monitor milestone goal. Each milestone agent should spawn one ticket worker per ticket under that milestone, collect worker reports, validate acceptance criteria, and report milestone status back.

## Acceptance

- Six milestone supervisor agents are spawned with the correct goal IDs, ticket IDs, delegation instructions, validation duties, and reporting expectations.

Update the Console alerts experience so notification logs expose the Core-supported type, channel, and event_type filters, and channel rows provide a manual test action for webhook and email alert destinations.

documents.title: Ticket: Complete alert route coverage and maintenance semantics
documents.content:
# Ticket: Complete alert route coverage and maintenance semantics

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Ticket ID | T-20260527-122906-3f09 |
| Project | orion |
| Goal | G-20260527-111600-870c |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T12:29:06+02:00 |

## Description

Close the remaining alert route validation gaps by adding API coverage for list/update/delete behavior and deciding whether maintenance-aware routing requires a first-class route field or an explicit documented non-goal.

## Acceptance

- Alert route list, update, delete, and dry-run paths have focused tests; any maintenance routing behavior is either implemented in Core/OpenAPI or documented as intentionally out of scope for this phase with Maat evidence.

documents.title: Ticket: Complete grouping policies and summary delivery

| Project | orion |
| Goal | G-20260527-111606-93ee |
| Status | active |
| Created | 2026-05-27T12:29:14+02:00 |

## Description

Finish alert grouping policy support by adding selectable grouping policy fields on routes, delayed grouped summary delivery, and delivery API exposure for alert_group_id.

## Acceptance

- Routes can select grouping behavior, grouped incidents can produce delayed summaries instead of only immediate suppression, alert_group_id is exposed in delivery responses/OpenAPI/SDK, and focused Core tests cover the policy and summary behavior.

## Description

Promote the alert payload helpers into configurable delivery behavior by applying webhook signatures when configured and adding HTML email rendering alongside plain text.
